One of the most underrated aspects of LeBron is that he stayed mostly healthy for a long career with this kind of athleticism and hard style of playing. Most guys who are this explosive and athletic much less at this size run into a major injury at some point. The fact he was like this consistently for over a decade is just mind boggling.

Yeah, most of Wilt's stories are pretty clearly untrue. His weightlifting stuff is almost certainly exaggerated (his reported number would have made him the second-strongest weightlifter in the world while casually doing reps without a warmup), and most of his track and field stuff is completely unverified. Hell, we've got records of college and highschool track for the time, and Wilt isn't anywhere to be found, despite most of his claimed numbers winning events.

Lebron lol. Besides Steph in 2016, prime LeBron was absurd. Basically a 2k my player/create a player dream. I already miss him and he’s still in the league but he’s old. A 6’8 athletic specimen who does not have durability issues and has no weaknesses on offense or defense… that’s what lebron was. 2018 was the most fun I had watching a playoff run for a single player in a long long time. Even though I already like watching Wemby and giannis was cool too, you can tell they are just too big and have durability issues. They also can’t handle the ball close to the way prime LeBron could. I still have yet to see a big forward or big man who did what LeBron did with ball handling, stamina, shooting, passing, defense…
Especially to a man like Pop who is so deeply invested in *life outside basketball*

> dog Chet This insult seems so obvious in retrospect and yet, I don’t think I’ve seen anybody use it yet. I myself feel like I’m dumb for not thinking of it first. I guess maybe this gives more credence to the argument from oldheads that fundamental moves only seem obvious and easy once they’ve already been invented. Being the first one to invent something before it exists is why the guys who played against “plumbers and milkmen” should be respected more.
